title: Why You Can't Hear A Star Explode From Earth

segments.text: A supernova is completely silent from our viewpoint.
Sound needs a medium to travel, like air or water.
Space is a vacuum, offering no medium for sound.
So, we witness the light but hear nothing.
That's why we rely on telescopes to study space.
Witnessing cosmic events is a visual sensation only.
Our universe is a silent theater of wonders.
Follow Curio for your daily dose of science.

description: The universe is silent, even at its loudest moments. Follow for daily science & tech! 🌌
